intent: Define a vendor-neutral telemetry contract for SecurityRecipes so agentic AI and MCP runs can be reconstructed from OpenTelemetry-shaped traces without leaking secrets, raw tenant context, or unredacted tool payloads into observability systems.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Security Engineering, Detection Engineering, GRC, model risk, procurement security, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.category: Agentic AI telemetry contract and secure trace evidence layer
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes makes agent observability reviewable: every governed run must emit enough trace, policy, context, egress, approval, receipt, and redaction evidence for enterprise reviewers to trust the run without reading transcripts.
positioning.why_now: MCP and agent frameworks are standardizing tool calls, handoffs, and GenAI traces at the same time enterprises are discovering that raw prompts, tool arguments, and model outputs can become a new sensitive-data sink. A secure context layer needs a trace contract that is useful to defenders and safe for regulated environments.

standards_alignment.name: OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions for Generative AI Systems
standards_alignment.coverage: Maps SecurityRecipes runtime evidence to GenAI operations such as invoke_agent, execute_tool, retrieval, generate_content, model client calls, and provider-neutral span attributes.

standards_alignment.name: OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions for Model Context Protocol
standards_alignment.coverage: Anchors MCP spans, JSON-RPC request identifiers, method names, session ids, protocol versions, transport attributes, and trace-context propagation through params._meta.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification
standards_alignment.coverage: Requires protected resource metadata, OAuth resource indicators, token audience validation, PKCE, scope challenges, short-lived tokens, and token-passthrough denial evidence.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Transports
standards_alignment.coverage: Covers stdio and Streamable HTTP transport requirements, Origin validation, localhost binding, authentication expectations, and session behavior that must appear in trace evidence.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Security Best Practices
standards_alignment.coverage: Supports SSRF-safe discovery, redirect validation, private IP blocking, session hijack resistance, local server hardening, and auditability for MCP client and server deployments.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
standards_alignment.coverage: Maps telemetry evidence to Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage expectations for GAI data provenance, monitoring, risk response, and lifecycle controls.

standards_alignment.name: CISA AI Data Security Best Practices
standards_alignment.coverage: Frames telemetry as an AI data-security boundary where confidentiality, integrity, monitoring, and defensive detection must be balanced against sensitive-data minimization.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications 2025
standards_alignment.coverage: Covers prompt injection, sensitive information disclosure, excessive agency, insecure output handling, supply-chain risk, and overreliance signals that trace contracts need to expose safely.

standards_alignment.name: CISA Secure by Design
standards_alignment.coverage: Connects the telemetry contract to secure-by-design product evidence: secure defaults, logging, SSO/MFA-compatible operations, transparency, and measurable customer security outcomes.

signal_classes.title: Agent Session Span
signal_classes.minimum_evidence: Session span links the run receipt, identity, tenant, and workflow before any model or tool child span is trusted.

signal_classes.title: Model Call Span
signal_classes.minimum_evidence: Model spans identify provider, model, operation, redaction state, and governing policy without storing raw prompts by default.

signal_classes.title: MCP Tool Call Span
signal_classes.minimum_evidence: MCP spans include JSON-RPC, session, protocol, transport, tool, policy, and authorization evidence before a tool result is trusted.

signal_classes.title: Secure Context Retrieval Span
signal_classes.minimum_evidence: Retrieval spans must prove source hashes, package hash, poisoning scan state, and retrieval decision before retrieved content influences an agent.

signal_classes.title: Runtime Policy Decision Event
signal_classes.minimum_evidence: Every policy decision records the policy pack hash, rule, gate phase, namespace, requested access mode, and allow/hold/deny/kill result.

signal_classes.title: Context Egress Decision Event
signal_classes.minimum_evidence: Egress events prove the destination, data class, policy hash, tenant, and decision before context reaches a model, MCP server, telemetry sink, or public corpus.

signal_classes.title: Human Approval Event
signal_classes.minimum_evidence: Approval events link high-impact or held actions to a named approval system, actor, expiry, and optional risk acceptance record.

signal_classes.title: Verifier Result Event
signal_classes.minimum_evidence: Verifier events bind tests, evals, red-team replay, or scanners back to the run receipt and artifact hash.

signal_classes.title: Agentic Incident Signal
signal_classes.minimum_evidence: Incident signals carry classification, severity, containment, replay, and correlation evidence so incidents do not require transcript archaeology.

redaction_tiers.description: Safe operational metadata such as workflow id, tool namespace, decision, and hashed artifact references.
Tenant, service, repository, source ids, and approval references that require internal access controls and retention policy.
Prompt text, model output, tool arguments, tool results, and retrieved context snippets that require explicit opt-in, redaction, and access-controlled retention.
Credentials, raw tokens, private keys, seed phrases, unredacted PII, and cross-tenant context that must kill the session if emitted.

telemetry_checks.title: Trace context propagates across agent, model, MCP, and verifier spans
pass_conditions: trace id and span id exist
correlation id links runtime decision and receipt evidence

telemetry_checks.title: MCP tool span includes JSON-RPC, session, transport, and policy attributes
pass_conditions: session id is present but not used as authentication
policy decision is linked to the tool call

telemetry_checks.title: Raw prompt, completion, tool arguments, and tool results are absent or redacted
pass_conditions: sensitive opt-in attributes are absent by default
captured prompts or tool payloads are redacted

telemetry_checks.title: Context retrieval links source hashes, poisoning scan, and package hash
pass_conditions: source ids and hashes are present

telemetry_checks.title: Egress telemetry proves destination, data class, policy hash, and decision
pass_conditions: egress decision links to a policy hash

telemetry_checks.title: Verifier output can be reconstructed from receipt and artifact hashes
Incident signal links severity, containment, replay, and correlation evidence
pass_conditions: containment and replay identifiers are present
correlation id links incident, run, and receipt evidence

telemetry_checks.title: Secret or cross-tenant telemetry kills the session
pass_conditions: kill decision is emitted when prohibited telemetry appears

enterprise_adoption_packet.board_level_claim: SecurityRecipes can make agentic telemetry useful for audit and detection without turning observability into a prompt, tool-argument, or secret exfiltration sink.
default_questions_answered: Which trace fields must exist before an agent run is trusted?
Which OpenTelemetry and MCP attributes should an enterprise collector require?
Which prompt, output, tool argument, and tool result fields are opt-in only?
Which telemetry findings force hold, deny, or kill-session decisions?
Which runtime fields connect traces to run receipts, context packs, egress decisions, and incidents?
enterprise_adoption_packet.recommended_first_use: Attach the generated telemetry contract to AI platform logging standards, MCP gateway requirements, SIEM onboarding, procurement security reviews, and acquisition diligence.
enterprise_adoption_packet.sales_motion: Lead with open trace contracts, then sell hosted collector policy, redaction verification, trace-to-receipt retention, SIEM export, and agentic incident evidence vaulting.

commercialization_path.open_layer: Public telemetry contract, generated evidence pack, deterministic evaluator, docs, and MCP tools for teams standardizing agent and MCP traces.
commercialization_path.team_layer: Customer-specific collector config, private attribute allowlists, redaction tests, retention matrices, and SIEM field mappings.
commercialization_path.enterprise_layer: Hosted trace ingestion, policy enforcement, redaction verification, receipt vaulting, SIEM/SOAR export, incident reconstruction, and trust-center telemetry APIs.
commercialization_path.acquirer_value: A frontier lab, cloud platform, or security vendor gets a standards-aligned telemetry control plane that makes agentic AI easier to monitor, approve, and sell into regulated enterprises.
